events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce JSON answer. Need assess scenario: manufacturing/assembly floor, structural ironwork, column/beam erection & plumb, rough-complete, observed condition: temporary bracing removed before decking/permanent stability. Work roughed in not closed out. Reference AISC 303, OSHA 1926 Subpart R. Treat as roughly 70 percent complete until defects corrected.

We need decide decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant: temporary bracing removed before decking/permanent stability is a stability/safety hazard. Risk high or critical? Removing temporary bracing before permanent stability can lead to structural collapse; in production floor with workers, high/critical. The prompt says treat as roughly 70 percent complete until defects corrected. Component condition installed-defective. Findings: temporary bracing removed prematurely, structure lacks permanent stability/decking, out-of-plumb/collapse risk, non-compliant with OSHA/AISC. Actions: reinstall temporary bracing, verify plumb and alignment, install permanent connections/decking/diaphragm, inspect by competent person, submit engineer approval before removing bracing. References: AISC 303 (Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges) bracing requirements, OSHA 1926 Subpart R (Steel Erection) §1926.706, §1926.752 (site layout), §1926.754 (structural steel assembly) maybe temporary bracing. Need include relevant anchors.

Need fill all fields. decision fail. risk critical maybe high. Since dangerous or non-compliant, fail. work_stage rough-complete. percent_complete 70. value 0. sound_source empty? Since not audio. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty list, workable empty list. component_conditions ["installed-defective"]. confidence 0.95. findings list. actions list. rationale. references.

Need ensure valid JSON, no extra prose.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":{"decision":"fail","risk":"critical","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective"],"percent_complete":70,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Temporary bracing was removed before decking or permanent stability was achieved.","The column/beam assembly is installed but not stable, creating a collapse/instability hazard.","Condition violates structural steel erection safety and stability requirements.","Work is not ready for close-out or load transfer until stability is restored."],"actions":["Stop work in the affected area and restrict access.","Reinstall required temporary bracing and guy lines to secure columns/beams.","Verify plumb, alignment, and connection tightness before removing any bracing.","Complete permanent connections, decking, and/or lateral bracing to achieve stability.","Have a competent person / structural engineer inspect and approve before bracing removal.","Document corrective action and re-inspect before proceeding."],"rationale":"Temporary bracing is required to maintain stability of steel columns and beams until permanent framing, decking, or lateral bracing provides adequate stability. Removing it prematurely creates a high risk of collapse and is non-compliant with AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R. The work is therefore failed and must be corrected before it can be considered complete or safe.","references":["AISC 303 - Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges (stability and bracing requirements)","OSHA 1926 Subpart R - Steel Erection (1926.754 Structural steel assembly; 1926.706 Stability requirements)"]}}
